pulse: Markets closed Friday April 17 at record highs after Iran briefly reopened the Strait of Hormuz, but the weekend brought whiplash: Tehran reclosed the strait on April 18, the US seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship on April 19, and the two-week ceasefire expires Wednesday April 22. US negotiators fly to Pakistan today for a critical second round of talks with Iran. Monday opens in risk-off mode with oil volatile near $96 Brent, gold near $4,878, and equities facing a binary geopolitical outcome by midweek.

movers.reason: Spain's benchmark led European gains on April 17 as ceasefire optimism boosted energy-exposed banks and the broader risk-on rally

movers.reason: Dow surged to 49,447 on April 17 as Iran's Hormuz reopening announcement triggered the biggest single-day oil price drop since 2022, lifting consumer and transport stocks

stocks: NASDAQ: GCL Global Holdings â€” high volatility
portfolio: Marc's SGD-based portfolio faces a week defined by the Iran ceasefire binary outcome (expires April 22). Gold at $4,878 is a clear outperformer on four consecutive weekly gains â€” consider whether to trim after extended run. BTC near $76K shows institutional demand recovering from the February crash. US equity positions (SPX at 7,126, NDX at 24,468) sit at record highs â€” the ceasefire expiry Wednesday is the key reversal catalyst; a breakdown could reprice both 5%+ lower as oil re-spikes toward $100+. IBEX35 at 18,485 is just off the February all-time high of 18,570; Spain's 2.2% projected GDP growth and strong banking sector underpin medium-term support. Oil/energy exposure faces the highest near-term risk â€” Pakistan talks today are pivotal. STI opened Monday at 4,975 (-0.30%), tracking regional caution; 4,950 is the near-term support. EUR/USD at 1.1765 is supportive for European holdings in SGD terms (EUR/SGD ~1.4922; USD/SGD ~1.268). GCL (NASDAQ) dropped 14.27% in a recent session at $0.80 â€” high volatility demands close monitoring. USD/THB at 31.92 shows baht among the weaker Asian currencies. Key action: review oil hedge sizing before Wednesday ceasefire deadline; gold position is performing; watch Pakistan talks for SPX/NDX re-rating trigger.

alerts.text: CRITICAL: Iran reclosed Strait of Hormuz on April 18. US seized Iranian cargo ship April 19. Ceasefire expires Wednesday April 22. US-Iran talks restart today in Pakistan â€” outcome this week drives oil, bonds, and global risk appetite.
Oil binary risk this week: ceasefire extension = Brent likely falls further; ceasefire collapse = Brent could re-spike above $100 and gold could push through $5,000.
GCL (NASDAQ: GCL) dropped 14.27% in a single session, trading at $0.80. Small-cap, high-volatility â€” review position size.
STI opens week at 4,975, down 0.3%, tracking regional caution. Watch 4,950 support level.
MIT Technology Review releases inaugural '10 Things That Matter in AI' list on April 21 â€” watch for AI sector moves.
IBEX35 at 18,485 near all-time high of 18,570 (Feb 27). Spain's economic fundamentals remain solid at 2.2% projected GDP growth.

news.title: Iran Reclosed Strait of Hormuz April 18 After 24-Hour Opening
news.text: In a dramatic reversal, Iran announced on April 18 that it was reimposing control of the Strait of Hormuz just one day after declaring it open for commercial shipping. Tehran said the closure would remain until the US fully lifts its naval blockade of Iranian ports. The US then seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the strait on April 19, further ratcheting tensions ahead of the April 22 ceasefire deadline.

news.title: US Negotiators Head to Pakistan for Second Round of Iran Talks
news.text: American diplomats departed for Pakistan on Monday April 20 to resume negotiations with Iranian counterparts. The ceasefire expires on Wednesday April 22, and Trump has publicly said he does not know whether it will be extended. A breakdown would likely trigger immediate oil market disruption.

news.title: Trump Rejects JD Vance's 20-Year Nuclear Enrichment Suspension Proposal
news.text: Vice President JD Vance proposed during the first round of talks a 20-year moratorium on Iran's uranium enrichment, but Trump publicly rejected this as insufficient, maintaining his demand for full dismantlement of Iran's nuclear program. The gap between the two sides on nuclear issues remains wide heading into today's Pakistan talks.

news.title: Oil Prices Crash 11% Then Recover in 24-Hour Hormuz Whiplash
news.text: May WTI crude collapsed $10.84, or 11.45%, on April 17 after Iran opened the Strait of Hormuz for non-Iranian commercial vessels. Brent fell to around $96. Within 24 hours Iran reversed the decision, triggering a partial recovery. The episode illustrates the extreme binary risk in energy markets ahead of the April 22 ceasefire deadline.

news.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow Close at Records on April 17
news.text: US equities extended their rally on April 17 to record closes: S&P 500 at 7,126 (+1.2%), Dow at 49,447 (+1.79%), and Nasdaq at 24,468 (+1.52%). CNBC noted it was the longest Nasdaq winning streak since 2009. The moves were fueled by Iran's Hormuz announcement before the weekend's reversal.

news.title: IMF April 2026 Outlook: 'Global Economy in the Shadow of War'
news.text: The IMF's World Economic Outlook published April 14 warns that the Middle East conflict has introduced rising commodity prices, firmer inflation expectations, and tighter financial conditions testing global resilience. The fund projects slower growth for most regions compared to January forecasts, with energy-importing economies most exposed.

news.title: Trump Threatens 50% Tariffs on Iran's Weapons Suppliers
news.text: On April 8, Trump threatened to impose 50% secondary tariffs on any country supplying military weapons to Iran, as part of a broader economic pressure campaign. Analysts questioned the legal basis and called the threat difficult to enforce in practice, but it added further uncertainty to global trade and alliance relationships.

news.title: Trump Family Middle East Financial Ties Under Scrutiny
news.text: The New York Times reported that Trump family members have financial ties to Middle Eastern executives who stand to benefit from Trump's signing of legislation repealing Syria sanctions. Jared Kushner's business partners â€” a Qatar-based family â€” secured $12 billion in Syrian reconstruction deals following the new law, raising conflict-of-interest questions.

news.title: The Economist: China Positioned to Win From US-Iran Conflict
news.text: The Economist's April 18-24 issue argues that Beijing stands to benefit strategically and economically from the US military entanglement in the Middle East. The cover references Napoleon's dictum about not interrupting enemies making mistakes, with articles examining how China's leverage has grown and US alliance credibility has been tested.

news.title: Nikkei 225 Hit Record 58,134 on April 16, Asia Retreats Monday
news.text: Japan's Nikkei 225 hit a record high of 58,134 on April 16 on hopes for a US-Iran diplomatic solution. Asian markets broadly retreated from those highs by April 17 as the Hormuz situation became more complex, and Monday's Asia session opened cautiously with STI at 4,975 and KOSPI under pressure.

news.title: Spain's IBEX35 Climbs 2.18% on April 17, Economy Outperforms Europe
news.text: Spain's main index rose 2.18% to 18,485 on April 17, close to its all-time high of 18,570 set on February 27. The government projects 2.2% GDP growth for 2026, supported by resilient domestic demand, labor market gains, and strong banking sector earnings. Banks contributed approximately 70% of the index's gains this year.

news.title: CNMC Opens 20 Sanction Proceedings Over Spain's April 2025 Blackout
news.text: Spain's National Markets and Competition Commission has launched 20 formal sanction proceedings linked to its investigation of the April 28, 2025 nationwide power blackout, according to El Economista. The investigation focuses on energy sector conduct and grid management responsibilities during the event.

news.title: Iberdrola Distributes 68,645 GWh in Q1 2026
news.text: Spanish utility Iberdrola reported distributing 68,645 gigawatt-hours of electricity in the first quarter of 2026, a strong result reflecting resilient energy demand in Spain despite the volatile global energy price environment driven by the Middle East conflict and Hormuz crisis.

news.title: OpenAI Closes $122 Billion Series D at $852 Billion Valuation
news.text: OpenAI completed its largest-ever funding round, closing a $122 billion Series D at an $852 billion valuation. The company has surpassed $25 billion in annualized revenue and is reportedly preparing for a potential IPO as early as late 2026. Rival Anthropic is approaching $19 billion in annualized revenue and also growing rapidly.

news.title: SpaceX Acquires xAI in $250 Billion Deal
news.text: Elon Musk's SpaceX reached an agreement to acquire xAI, the developer of the Grok AI models, in a transaction valued at $250 billion. The deal consolidates Musk's AI and space ventures under one corporate structure, creating a combined entity with capabilities spanning large language models, satellite internet, and rocket technology.

day_ahead: Monday April 20: The dominant event is the US-Iran negotiation round in Pakistan â€” any indication of ceasefire extension or breakdown will move oil, gold, and risk assets sharply. No major US economic data is scheduled for today. Asian markets are trading in risk-off mode with STI at 4,975 and KOSPI under pressure. European futures are expected to open cautiously given the weekend's Hormuz whiplash. Energy sector moves will be the leading indicator. Watch also for any diplomatic statements from Pakistan, Tehran, or Washington ahead of the April 22 ceasefire deadline. MIT Technology Review publishes its inaugural '10 Things That Matter in AI' list tomorrow April 21.

ai_news.title: OpenAI Closes $122B Series D at $852 Billion Valuation
ai_news.text: OpenAI secured a $122 billion Series D funding round, valuing the company at $852 billion â€” the highest private valuation in history. With over $25 billion in annualized revenue and reported IPO preparations for late 2026, OpenAI has established a commanding lead as the world's most valuable AI company. Anthropic follows at approaching $19 billion ARR and growing rapidly.

ai_news.title: SpaceX Acquires xAI for $250 Billion
ai_news.text: Elon Musk merged xAI â€” the developer of Grok AI models â€” into SpaceX in a deal valued at $250 billion. The combined entity will operate with capabilities spanning large language models, satellite internet infrastructure via Starlink, and rocket technology, creating a unique vertically integrated AI-and-infrastructure conglomerate.

ai_news.title: Adobe and NVIDIA Partner on Next-Generation Firefly AI Models
ai_news.text: Adobe and NVIDIA announced a collaboration to develop the next generation of Adobe Firefly generative AI models using NVIDIA GPUs, CUDA-X, and NeMo AI libraries. The partnership targets professional creative tools with best-in-class precision for image, video, and 3D content generation, announced in early April 2026.

ai_news.title: MIT Technology Review Releases '10 Things That Matter in AI' List Tomorrow
ai_news.text: MIT Technology Review will publish its inaugural annual list '10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now' on April 21, 2026. Designed to cut through hype and focus on near-term real-world impact, the list is expected to influence sector sentiment and investment narratives across AI-exposed equities.

ai_news.title: PwC: Top 20% of Companies Capture 75% of AI Economic Value
ai_news.text: A PwC global AI performance study published in April 2026 found that three-quarters of AI's economic gains are flowing to just the top quintile of companies â€” those deploying AI for growth and new revenue streams rather than pure productivity improvements. The finding signals accelerating winner-takes-most dynamics across the AI economy.
